title: Quiet spaces at the Southbank Campus
excerpt: Quiet spaces at the Southbank Campus Searching for a quiet space on campus?The Southbank Campus is home to diverse and dynamic creative learning. Noisy work often occurs from our studios, classrooms, and performances spaces, ranging from live music to workshop construction noise.Quiet spaces are designated areas where there is unlikely to be excessive noise. Two of these spaces are bookable.Flexible spaces are common areas that may be quiet at different times of the day.Spaces will have peak activity periods so you may need to try a few different spots before you find one that suits your needs

title: Employability in Fine Arts and Music
excerpt: Employability in Fine Arts and Music Your curriculum is at the heart of your employabilityOur courses provide a foundation for developing your employability. Every subject, assessment, placement, and activity helps you apply theory to practice, build confidence, and demonstrate your capabilities to future employers.In the Faculty of Fine Arts and Music, employability skills are woven into the fabric of our courses. Our creative community supports you to build peer networks, develop your practice, grow industry networks and explore creative collaborations. Your employability journey starts now

title: Big Band
excerpt: Big Band About Big BandThe Melbourne University Big Band (directed by Mat Jodrell) utilises a wide variety of styles and eras of the Big Band catalog, incorporating many current composers in its repertoire. The band is primarily a reading band, promoting the skills of sight reading and instant interpretation. The ensemble also uses improvisation as a key element, often featuring extended solos. This group performs works by composers such as Maria Schneider, Thad Jones, Gil Evans, Jim McNeely, Toshiko Akiyoshi, Bob Mintzer, John Clayton and Bob Brookmeyer to name a few. It also encourages compo

title: Chamber Music
excerpt: Chamber Music group application About Chamber MusicThe Melbourne Conservatorium of Music has a long and distinguished history of offering outstanding chamber music experience for all levels of students. The Chamber Music class is one of the few performance subjects at the MCM in which students of all instruments and levels come together in an exciting and supportive environment for vibrant musical exchange and appreciation.Students form their own chamber music groups at the beginning of the semester. title: Percussion Ensemble
excerpt: Percussion Ensemble MYRIAD is the Percussion Ensemble of the Melbourne Conservatorium of Music.Established in 2020 and directed by Head of Percussion Brent Miller, MYRIAD is named in honour of the esteemed inaugural Head of Percussion, Barry Quinn, in acknowledgement of his legacy.Each semester, MYRIAD presents concerts featuring diverse and eclectic programmes that showcase seminal repertoire, timeless classics, and works by staff and student composers, which are often world premieres.Proudly championing Australian repertoire, MYRIAD also dedicates time each year in the studio to record new A

title: Instrument Loans | Faculty of Fine Arts & Music | UniMelb
excerpt: Conservatorium instrument loansThe Melbourne Conservatorium of Music has a selection of musical instruments available for loan to current FAM students, including strings, woodwinds, brass and early music instruments for use in course-related activities. All instrument loans are subject to the recommendation of your teacher and approval of the responsible academic staff member and organized by the Technical Resources – Music & Instrumentation Department.All instrument loans require payment of a $250 refundable deposit, which must be paid prior to collection. title: Enrolment for research students
excerpt: EnrolmentGraduate Research students cannot self-enrol. When you first commenced your course, staff at the Faculty Research Office enrolled you in your course. Enrolment changesDuring your candidature you may need to make changes to your enrolment. This can include changing between part time and full time, taking a leave of absence, taking leave to study away or changing supervisors. excerpt: Graduate research seminarStudents enrolled in Music Performance and Musicology/Ethnomusicology are expected to participate in graduate seminars.The Graduate Research Seminar is held weekly, every Thursday during teaching semester.The seminar is a joint seminar.DetailsSeminars are held at 11am each Thursday during semesterSeminars are held in Room 721 Ian Potter Southbank Centre, Building 880, Sturt St Southbank (see map)Composition Seminar is held on Wednesday at 3.15pm in the Teaching Studio in Room 810, Ian Potter Southbank Centre, Building 880, Sturt St Southbank (see map)Seminar ScheduleSe

title: MCM Director's Writing Up Award
excerpt: Melbourne Conservatorium of MusicConservatorium Director’s Awards for Exceptional Doctoral ResearchThe Melbourne Conservatorium Director’s Awards for Exceptional Doctoral Research are granted twice per year on a competitive basis to support outstanding Conservatorium PhD students to produce high quality research outputs closely following submission of the PhD thesis or portfolio for examination.Applicants submit a proposal to extend their PhD research project with additional outputs in the public domain. A competitive proposal will typically involve two or three significant and distinct output

title: Current student info: Master of Music (Research)
excerpt: Master of Music (Research) in Music Performance Candidates undertake a supervised project that leads to a live, public recital consisting of at least 70 minutes of repertoire (plus an interval of 10-20 minutes) supported by an integrated written work of between 8,000 and 10,000 words. The related written project must be presented in a form that can be sent with the recorded performance for assessment by external examiners.Candidates are expected to participate in Performance Class and Seminars and are required to present one lecture-recital during their candidature.Course OutlineSupervision an
